source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Three rings are connected to each other as shown. Which of the following pictures also shows three rings connected in the same way?
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field only contains choice labels without option text, so no reliable choice_map was extracted
answer was kept as the original label because no option text mapping is available

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown' and asks which picture matches the same connection pattern
the task depends on visual/spatial relationships among rings rather than text-only facts
multi_step: solver must first infer the connection pattern in the reference image, then compare that pattern against the answer choices
the task is relational/topological matching rather than direct object recognition
there are 5 choices, so the visual relation must be checked across multiple candidate pictures
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single labeled answer 'D'
choices are explicitly enumerated from A to E
image asset exists and has good recorded readability_score (0.8808), supporting downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
registry passed with no recorded issues
high image_dependency_score and the required image asset exists, making the sample usable as an image-dependent problem
high verifiability_score with a single multiple-choice answer supports reliable downstream checking
the visual_topology_equivalence flag indicates task difficulty/risk but not missing core semantics
